statement: In patients presenting 4.5-9 h from LKW or with wake-up stroke, IV thrombolysis (alteplase or tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg) selected by perfusion or DWI-FLAIR mismatch is reasonable when EVT is not indicated or available.
caveats: Imaging-based selection is required; unselected late-window thrombolysis is not supported (TWIST).
Time from LKW remains a key safety determinant; TIMELESS extended TNK to 24 h with mismatch selection but the overall primary endpoint did not reach significance.

statement: In patients with anterior-circulation LVO and ASPECTS 3-5 (or core 50-100 mL) within 24 h of LKW, EVT is recommended to improve functional outcome.
caveats: Pre-stroke mRS, life expectancy, and goals of care still inform shared decision-making.
ASPECTS <3 was not adequately enrolled in the seminal RCTs and is a Class IIb area.

statement: In patients with anterior-circulation LVO presenting 6-24 h from LKW, EVT is recommended for those meeting DAWN or DEFUSE-3 imaging-selection criteria.
caveats: Outside trial-selection criteria, EVT is reasonable on a case-by-case basis with shared decision-making.
